news.title: ASX lower as banks slide; South32 jumps on $8.1b deal
news.snippet: The Australian sharemarket has fallen at the open with the big four banks weighing on the index but mining stocks rebounded.

news.title: West Wits banks first local funds for South African gold push
news.snippet: West Wits Mining has secured financial close and a first A$29.9 million drawdown on its senior loan facility to fully fund its flagship South African gold project.

news.title: Trump has made one of the world’s most important markets a mystery
news.snippet: The greatest oil shock in history seems to have passed with only fleeting effects as prices plunge. It doesn’t really add up.

news.title: Beetaloo closes in on Top End production with strong gas flows
news.snippet: Beetaloo Energy has hit its stride with strong gas flows at its Carpentaria-5H well in the Top End and construction of its pilot plant on schedule for later this year.

news.title: Kaiser fires up Tassie gold, base metals hunt with new exploration gun
news.snippet: Kaiser Reef has appointed an exploration manager to spearhead a major regional gold and base metals hunt across its richly endowed Henty project area.

news.title: Consultants will fight change ‘tooth and nail,’ warns senator who blew the whistle on KPMG, PwC
news.snippet: Senator Deborah O’Neill, a key figure in the PwC tax scandal and KPMG whistleblower scandal, says the culture inside global consulting giants ‘needs to be disrupted.’

news.title: Quick hits: Mexico madness at the Azteca, big Salah injury update
Karl Stefanovic dumped from radio show with Eddie McGuire after two episodes
news.snippet: The broadcaster is set to lose a weekly radio show after he was dumped from Nine following a controversial podcast with British far-right activist Tommy Robinson.

news.title: Who is Maya Joint, Australian conqueror of Serena at Wimbledon?
De Minaur one of only two Aussie men through to Wimbledon second round
Australian rising star takes down Serena Williams in Wimbledon return
